channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: First Friday Ice Cream Social (Jul 2, 2026)
item.description:
The Ice cream social will feature a display discussing future plans of the Pope Houde Museum, South Park Heritage Trail, and John P. "Top" Greene African American Cultural Center.



Location: Pope House Museum
Date: Aug. 7
Time: 5:30 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All 
Cost: Free

item.title: Business Bloom (Jul 2, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks is excited to announce the launch of the third annual Business Bloom program! Business Bloom brings entrepreneurs together to learn, share ideas, and grow their businesses. It is is open to everyone, including newcomers to Raleigh and the US. Build the skills, confidence, and local connections you need to start. 



Business Bloom 2026 will provide participants with six weeks of: 



Please apply if you: 



Apply today: Business Bloom Application ! If you have any questions, please email coeprogram@raleighnc.gov with the subject line “Business Bloom.”



This program is generously supported by Raleigh’s Office of Strategy and Innovation, with curriculum by global startup incubator Five One Labs. Business Bloom also benefits from an incredible network of local supporters and mentors.

item.title: Updates to Non-Resident Rental and Program Fees (Jun 24, 2026)
item.description:
To continue providing the high‑quality parks and recreation services our community enjoys, Raleigh Parks is updating how non‑resident fees are applied for rentals and programs.  



Your residency status is based on the household address listed in your RecLink account.



A 25% non‑resident fee will be added to new rental bookings for:



A 50% non‑resident fee will be added to new rental bookings for these special event venues:



These fees apply only to new bookings made on or after July 1. Existing reservations will stay the same unless changes are made after the effective date. If a change is made, the appropriate non‑resident fee will be added if the account does not meet resident criteria.  



A 25% non‑resident fee will be applied to all program registrations, replacing the current $15 flat fee.



If you believe your residency status is incorrect or you were mistakenly charged a non‑resident fee, we’re happy to help. Please contact the Recreation Business Office at RBO.registration@raleighnc.gov or 919‑996‑4800.

item.title: Raleigh Parks Fall Youth Athletics Registration (Jun 24, 2026)
item.description:
Programs will be offered at various park locations throughout the city. Registration will be held from July 1-14. Interested athletes may register online or at any City of Raleigh community center.



The Athletics Program will provide all general equipment and uniforms to registered athletes. To determine your child's league age, please refer to the age chart. 



For general information about youth athletics, please visit Athletics Frequently Asked Questions. For additional questions, please get in touch with Athletics at 919-996-6836 or email athletics@raleighnc.gov.



Registration: Wednesday, July 1 - Tuesday, July 14 
Ages: 5 - 17
Cost: $65 (Raleigh residents), $81.25 (non-Raleigh residents)
Registration will open on RecLink on July 1

item.title: Tour The City Greenhouse for Pollinator Week! (Jun 16, 2026)
item.description:
We will discuss how to choose native plants for your ecotype to support our beautiful, biodiverse ecosystem web, and each participant will leave with a greenhouse-grown plant. Registration required.



Location: The City Greenhouse (4225 Daly Rd., Raleigh, NC 27604)
Date: Friday, June 26
Time: 9 - 10:30 a.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: Free
Register for the Tour

item.title: View the Raleigh Parks 2025-2026 Annual Report (Jul 9, 2026)
item.description:
Like this year's Park and Recreation Month theme, “The Power Of,” the report celebrates the many ways parks and recreation brings us together, strengthens our health and well-being, and builds more resilient, connected communities.



The report also highlights our many significant accomplishments over the past year, including achieving national CAPRA accreditation, the opening of Gipson Play Plaza at Dorothea Dix Park, and implementation of the 2022 Raleigh Parks Bond.

item.title: Fleet Management Operations: The engine that keeps Raleigh running (Jul 7, 2026)
item.description:
The Fleet team works with departments across the City to reduce fuel use through initiatives like:



The City has about 2,500 vehicles on the road, 28 percent of those are either hybrid or electric. Fleet is also leading the way in charging infrastructure by installing the first 200-kW Direct Current Fast Charger (DCFC), owned and operated by the City, at our Westinghouse Blvd fueling hub.  



Renewable propane is a plant-based fuel made from vegetable oils and fats, and compared to fossil fuels, it produces far less greenhouse gas emissions.
The 10 newly converted pickup trucks will join the ranks of other renewable propane-fueled City vehicles and machines throughout the City. Currently, the City has 94 assets that run on this fuel, including a PRCR garbage truck and six flatbed dump trucks. 



Using renewable propane in City vehicles significantly reduces our demand for conventional fossil fuels. One more way the City is working to reduce the community’s greenhouse gas emissions by 80 percent by 2050.

item.title: Soccer Fan Fest at Pullen Park (Jun 15, 2026)
item.description:
Join us for two live screenings of the 2026 World Cup!



News Published 6/15/2026 Updated 6/15/2026



Watch back-to-back games featuring Canada vs. Switzerland and Scotland vs. Brazil. Bring your friends, family, and your own lawn chair or blanket to watch the beautiful game.



Participate in lawn games and soccer-themed inflatables before and during the games! The Pullen Park carousel and train will be operating until 8 pm. Refreshments will be available for purchase at the Pullen Café.



Date: Wednesday, June 24
Time: 2:30 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost Free



3 - 5 p.m. | Canada vs. Switzerland
6 - 8 p.m. | Scotland vs. Brazil



June 24, 2026 2:30PM



Pullen Park Amusements
520 Ashe Ave
Raleigh, NC 27606



919-996-6468



Pullen Park Amusements



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Intro to Hosting on Zoom (Jul 8, 2026)
item.description:
Thinking about hosting a group video call? Thinking about hosting your own Zoom meeting? Come to this lecture for an overview of hosting Zoom meetings. In this introductory lecture, we’ll go over what you need to start your first meeting, as well as some hosting tips to make it a success. You’ll also learn about special features for hosts, including ways Zoom keeps your meetings secure. 



Free on-site parking is available. Contact Daisie Blue with any questions at 919-307-1968.



Location: Raleigh Pathways Center
(900 S. Wilmington Street, Raleigh, NC 27601)
Date: Monday, Aug 17 and Thursday, August 20
Time: 10:50 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: July is Park and Recreation Month (Jun 30, 2026)
item.description:
The City of Raleigh proudly joins communities across the country in proclaiming July as Park and Recreation Month, celebrating the vital role parks and recreation play in building strong, vibrant, and resilient communities.



For over 40 years, Park and Recreation Month has been a time to honor the more than 160,000 full-time professionals—along with the hundreds of thousands of part-time, seasonal staff, and volunteers—who maintain and support our nation's parks and recreation programs.



Throughout July, Raleigh Parks will celebrate the power of parks to enhance health, support equity, protect the environment, and bring people together. This national initiative is led by the National Recreation and Park Association (NRPA).



We invite you to explore programs, spaces, and services that make Raleigh’s parks a cornerstone of our community.



To learn more about Park and Recreation Month and how to get involved, visit nrpa.org/July and follow the conversation online using #ThePowerOf.



City of Raleigh Proclaims July as Parks and Recreation Month

item.title: Learn on the Go Kayak Skills and Kayak Tours (Jun 22, 2026)
item.description:
Discover kayaking at Falls Lake! Whether you are brand new to paddle sports or looking to sharpen your skills, this class has you covered. Our certified instructors will guide you through the basics, including how to launch your vessel, proper posture, paddle strokes, and self-rescue all while touring the scenic shoreline. All equipment is provided. Preregistration is required.



Details
Saturday, Aug. 1, 8 - 11 a.m.
Ages: 16 and older
Cost: $75
Register on RecLink



Explore Falls Lake on a fun, beginner-friendly kayak tour! Join us for a 2-hour paddle trip along the park's scenic northern shoreline. We will meet at the waterfront, go over basic instructions, and launch together. Guidance and all necessary paddle equipment are provided. Preregistration is required.



Dates and Times
Saturday, July 18, 6 - 8 p.m.
Friday, July 31, 6 - 8 p.m.
Sunday, Aug. 2, 6 - 8 p.m.
Friday, Aug. 28, 6 - 8 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $30
Register on RecLink

item.title: Standup Paddleboard Skills Classes and Tours (Jun 22, 2026)
item.description:
Whether you are brand new to paddleboarding or looking to sharpen your skills, this class has you covered. Our certified instructors will guide you through the basics, including how to launch your vessel, proper posture, paddle strokes, and self-rescue, all while touring the scenic shoreline. All equipment is provided. Preregistration is required.



Dates and Times
Saturday, June 27, 8 - 11 a.m.
Saturday, July 25, 8 - 11 a.m
Ages: 16 and older
Cost: $75
Register on RecLink



Explore Falls Lake on a fun, beginner-friendly standup paddleboard tour! Join us for a 2-hour paddle trip along the park's scenic northern shoreline. We will meet at the waterfront, go over basic instructions, and launch together. Guidance and all necessary paddle equipment are provided. Preregistration is required.



Dates and Times
Sunday, June 28, 9 - 11 a.m. 
Wednesday, July 15, 6 - 8 p.m.
Friday, July 24, 6 - 8 p.m. 
Friday, Aug.7, 6 - 8 p.m. 
Sunday, Aug. 23, 6 - 8 p.m. 
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $30
Register on RecLink

item.title: Yoga in the Galleries (Jun 22, 2026)
item.description:
Bring your own mat, tools, and water. Taught by Elizabeth Comtois, registered yoga teacher and founder of Embodied Yoga LLC.



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Sunday, July 19
Time: 2 - 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: First Friday at the City of Raleigh Museum (Jun 22, 2026)
item.description:
Join us at the Museum for special evening hours from 6 - 9 p.m. to engage with our new exhibit, make a themed craft, and experience a night at the museum!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Friday, July 3
Time: 6 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Juneteenth Programs and Events (May 29, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 5/29/2026 Updated 6/22/2026



Join Raleigh Parks for a variety of programs and activities for the whole family.



Juneteenth is on Friday, June 19. Visit Holiday Parks Facility Closures.



Pope House Museum
Date: Now - June 21
Saturdays, 10 a.m. & 2 p.m.
Sundays, 2 p.m. 
Ages: All

item.title: Pages in the Park (May 29, 2026)
item.description:

News Published 5/29/2026 Updated 5/29/2026

Improvements are funded by the 2022 Parks Bond. Learn more about the Leesville Community Park Improvements.



Date: Tuesday, June 9
Time: 10:30 a.m.
Ages: All

June 9, 2026 10:30AM

Raleigh, NC 27613

item.title: Moore Square Soccer Watch Party (Jul 7, 2026)
item.description:
Experience every goal, save, and celebration alongside fellow fans at the World Cup Final Watch Party. Food and drinks will be available for purchase, including beer and wine.



Location: Moore Square
Date: Sunday, July 19
Time: 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Help Shape the Future of Biltmore Hills Park (Jun 29, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 6/29/2026 Updated 6/29/2026



Hosted at the playground, the event will feature popcorn, snow cones, games, and a cooled indoor area to learn more about proposed improvements.



All voices are welcome as the project team continues developing a community‑informed vision.



Location: Biltmore Hills Park
(2615 Fitzgerald Drive, Raleigh, NC 27610)
Date: Saturday, July 18
Time: 10 a.m. - 12 p.m. 
Ages: All
Cost: Free



July 18, 2026 10:00AM

2615 Fitzgerald Drive

item.title: Improve Access to Arts, Parks, Cultural, and Recreational Programs (Jul 6, 2026)
item.description:
We want to understand where we can provide more programming to better suit residents' needs, and the best ways to make sure more people know about programs going on in their neighborhood.



This survey is part of Raleigh’s Strategic Plan that was developed based on City Council’s vision and resident feedback to improve quality of life in Raleigh across several key areas.



To provide your feedback, visit engage.raleighnc.gov



Provide Feedback

item.title: Juggling Record-Breaking Attempt (Jul 6, 2026)
item.description:
Professional juggler, Chris “Juggleboy” Fowler, will attempt to break the juggling record for catching five full-sized baseball bats the greatest number of times in one minute!



The current record is 121 catches in one minute, so you don’t want to miss this challenging feat. This free event will take place on the Pullen event stage. Bring your friends and family to spend a unique afternoon at the park.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Date: Saturday, July 25
Time: 2 - 4 p.m., Record-breaking attempt at 3:30
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Best of Pullen Park Summer Camp! (Jun 4, 2026)
item.description:
Pullen Park Community Center



News Published 6/4/2026 Updated 6/4/2026



This unique camp gives children the opportunity to experience everything that makes Pullen Park such a special place. Based at Pullen Community Center, campers will spend their week exploring the park, making a splash with indoor swimming at the aquatic center, enjoying amusement rides, playing on the playground, learning new skills through daily tennis lessons, creating crafts, and participating in fun camp games and activities.



Every day is packed with movement, adventure, laughter, and opportunities to make new friends. No tennis racquet is required; we provide everything needed for a great week of fun.



Reach out to Pullen Community Center Director, AG Osborne at AG.Osborne@raleighnc.gov, with any questions.



Location: Pullen Park Community Center
Dates & Ages: 
Week 1: June 15 - 18 (Ages 7 - 9) 
Week 2: June 22 - 25 (Ages 10 - 12) 
Cost: $250 resident fee, $265 non-resident 
Register on RecLink



Pullen Community Center
408 Ashe Ave
Raleigh, NC 27607



AG.Osborne@raleighnc.gov
919-996-6052



Pullen Park Community Center

item.title: Common or Complicated… One Thread Dance Workshop (Jun 11, 2026)
item.description:
Through storytelling, movement, and collaborative artmaking, participants will explore personal histories, family journeys, and their own experiences in America through creative expression while contributing meaningful textile items such as fabric, scarves, T-shirts, and blankets. These collected materials may be incorporated as movement props or assembled into a temporary collaborative art installation featured during the final informal sharing. Together, participants will help weave a living reflection of memory, identity, resilience, and connection. Registration is required.

Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, July 18
Time: 10 a.m. - 1 p.m.
Ages: 15 and older
Cost: $31
Register on RecLink

item.title: Strickland Road Park Celebration (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 6/3/2026 Updated 6/4/2026



Enjoy music, refreshments, and a special visit from the Raleigh Police Department Mounted Patrol.



Learn more about the Strickland Road Park Improvements.



Date: Wednesday, June 24
Time: 3 - 5 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



June 24, 2026 3:00PM



12804 Strickland Road
Raleigh, NC 27613



Strickland Road Neighborhood Park

item.title: First Friday Market and Movie (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
This free monthly event brings the community together for local vendors, food trucks, live music, and a featured outdoor movie in the heart of downtown Raleigh.



Join us on the first Friday of each month from May through November, with the market and food trucks beginning at 5 p.m., live music at 5:30 p.m., and the movie starting around 7:15 p.m.



Market & Food Trucks: 5 - 9 p.m.
Live Music: 5:30 p.m.
Movie:  7:15 p.m.*
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Presented in partnership with Get 2 Know Raleigh. Movie sponsored by the Downtown Raleigh Alliance.

item.title: Foundations: Art & Expression at Latta (Jun 3, 2026)
item.description:
Join us as we celebrate African-American history, creativity, and community through live performance, visual art, and local vendors. Set against the historic landscape of Latta, where past and present meet.



5 p.m. | Tarish Pipkins - Jeghetto
5:45 p.m. | The Magic of African Rhythm - Drum & dance
6:45 p.m. | Dasan Ahanu - Spoken word
7:25 p.m. | Brenda Bailey - Spoken word & song



Location: Latta University Historic Park 
(1001 Parker Street, Raleigh, NC 27607)
Date: Saturday, June 27 
Time: 4 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Raleigh Parks Launches Solar-Powered Lighting Pilot (Jun 26, 2026)
item.description:
In May 2026, five solar-powered bollard lights were installed along the boardwalk section of the trail located between Capital Boulevard and N. Raleigh Boulevard. The greenway system currently operates from dawn until dusk.

While this schedule works well during the long days of summer, it means significantly reduced usable hours during winter months, a limitation that trail users have consistently highlighted. Community members have expressed a desire for lighting along the trails so that access during winter can mirror the operating hours during the summer months, allowing safe use of the trails for commuting or recreation before or after working hours.



Adding traditional electrical infrastructure to Raleigh’s greenway network presents many challenges. Many trail segments traverse remote, natural areas where running electrical conduit would be extraordinarily costly and logistically complex. Solar power offers a possible solution, delivering reliable, renewable energy exactly where it’s needed, without the expense or disruption of trenching and wiring. The five bollard lights installed on the Crabtree Creek Trail boardwalk are entirely solar powered, harvesting energy from the sun throughout the day and storing it for use after dark.

item.title: Cheer on Team USA with Soccer Watch Parties! (Jun 26, 2026)
item.description:
Join us at Moore Square for a World Cup Watch Party as Team USA takes on Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16 this Monday, July 6, at 8 P.M. Food and drinks available for purchase, including beer and wine.



We'll be showing every Team USA match as long as they continue advancing through the tournament, giving fans a place to rally behind the red, white, and blue throughout their World Cup run. And no matter who earns a spot in the championship, we'll also host a World Cup Final Watch Party on Sunday, July 19.



*Team USA Watch Parties are contingent upon Team USA advancing in the World Cup. The World Cup Final Watch Party will be held regardless of which teams qualify.



Admission is free. Bring your team spirit, grab a bite to eat, enjoy a cold drink, and help cheer Team USA all the way to the championship!

item.title: Enjoy a Trip to the Casino or a Baseball Game (Jun 18, 2026)
item.description:
Thursday, Oct. 15, or Friday, Nov. 20



Get ready to dive into an electrifying 24/7 gaming extravaganza at Danville Casino, where excitement knows no bounds! Unleash your inner thrill-seeker at one of the 79 table games, 1,500 slot machines all poised to catapult you into a world of non-stop entertainment and epic wins.



The Casino is fully open. Enjoy lunch on your own at Ramsay’s Kitchen and Dan Dan Noodle Bar, which tempts taste buds with decadent fare, while Three Stacks, 500 Block, and Caesar Eats provide local specialties and American favorites in a casual setting all day long. Grab your favorite coffee drink and breakfast sandwich from the convenient onsite Starbucks or pick up snacks and sodas from the Caesars Essentials store. You must be 21 years old with a valid government ID.



Dates: Thursday, Oct. 15, or Friday, Nov. 20
Time: 9 a.m. to 6 p.m.
Cost: $30 / Residents, $45 / Non-residents



Visit our website to learn more about our day and multi-day travel opportunities.

item.title: Iceland Awaits: Reserve Your Spot Before It's Gone (Jun 18, 2026)
item.description:
September 16 - 22 and October 7 - 12



News Published 6/18/2026 Updated 6/18/2026



The Raleigh Parks Active Adult Program, in partnership with Premier World Discovery, is excited to offer two unforgettable journeys to Iceland. Experience breathtaking landscapes, majestic waterfalls, and remarkable geothermal wonders while exploring one of the world's most unique destinations. 



While these trips are designed with older adults in mind, all adults are welcome to participate. Discover new places, build lasting friendships, and enjoy stress-free travel with the support of our experienced Trip Assistants every step of the way.



Discover the land of fire and ice on this unforgettable journey through Iceland’s breathtaking landscapes, vibrant culture, and fascinating history, all while enjoying five nights in one hotel in Reykjavik, the world’s northernmost capital. Tour Highlights include round-trip airfare from Raleigh, Reykjavik City Tour, Blue Lagoon, Golden Circle Tour, Strokkur Geyser & Gullfoss/Golden Falls, Thingvellir National Park, South Coast Tour, Skogar Folk Museum, Skogafoss Waterfall, Keflavik & Bessastadir, Viking Ship Museum, Lake Kleifarvatn. 



Carmen Livingston 
Carmen.Livingston@raleighnc.gov
919-996-4734



Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults



Jun 18, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Raleigh Parks Celebrates Pollinator Week 2026 (Jun 18, 2026)
item.description:
Pollinators are in decline worldwide due to habitat loss and climate change. With over 10,000 acres of green space, 220 parks, and 120 miles of greenway trail, Raleigh Parks and Natural Resources has a great opportunity to support and protect our pollinators. As you are out and about, look for our pollinator protection signs, located at our wildflower plantings throughout the city.

The City of Raleigh is also a Bee City USA ! We reach out to our broader community to foster support for pollinators by hosting events, engaging with volunteers, and creating pollinator habitat each year.

If you would like to help support our pollinator protection efforts, sign up for volunteer opportunities today!

item.title: Viewing the Revolutionary Age through an LGBTQ+ Lens (Jun 10, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 6/10/2026 Updated 6/10/2026



Megan L. Cherry is an associate professor at North Carolina State University who specializes in seventeenth- and eighteenth-century North American history. This lecture will take a look at several lesser-known Americans from the Revolutionary era to show that sexuality and gender were being contested at the same time as our war for independence.



Location: City of Raleigh Museum 
Date: Thursday, July 16
Time: 7 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free 



July 16, 2026 7:00PM



City of Raleigh Museum
220 Fayetteville Street (Floor 1)
Raleigh, NC 27601 



919-996-2220



City of Raleigh Museum



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Sertoma Arts Center Temporarily Closing for Improvements (Jun 2, 2026)
item.description:
While the facility is closed for construction, many arts classes and programs will continue at alternate Raleigh Parks locations so participants can continue creating during the project. Information about relocated programming and registration will be shared directly with participants and available online.



Sertoma Arts Center is located at Shelley Lake Park at 1400 W. Millbrook Road. 



The Sertoma Arts Center Improvements Project will modernize and expand the facility to better serve Raleigh’s growing arts community. 



The project is funded through the 2022 Parks Bond and is designed to improve both the visitor experience and the functionality of the facility for artists, instructors, and program participants.



For decades, Sertoma Arts Center has provided opportunities for creativity, learning, and community connection through classes, exhibitions, studio programs, and cultural experiences for all ages.



These improvements will help preserve the center’s role as an important community arts space while expanding opportunities for future programming and improving accessibility for visitors of all abilities.



Raleigh Parks appreciates the community’s patience and support as this investment helps prepare Sertoma Arts Center for future generations of artists and visitors.



For updates, visit the Project webpage or follow Raleigh Parks on social media.

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture: Moving the NC Museum of History's Collection (Jun 25, 2026)
item.description:
In preparation for their renovation construction project at the museum, the entire artifact collection had to be removed from the building!



NC Museum of History Collections Section Chief John Campbell will share some of the challenges the staff faced in moving 90,000+ objects safely in a relatively short period of time, and how they accomplished this feat.

item.title: Stars, Stripes, and Grandkids (Jun 9, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 6/9/2026 Updated 6/9/2026



Join us at Kiwanis Park for a red-white-and-blue goodie hunt, sparkling snacks, story time and hands-on holiday crafts. Explore all the activity stations, make memories together, and enjoy an afternoon packed with laughter, creativity, and love!



Location: Kiwanis Park
Date: Wednesday, July 1
Time: 4 - 6 p.m.
Ages: Children 12 years & under/Grandparents
Cost: Children - $3 | Adults - Free 
Get Tickets on RecLink



July 1, 2026 4:00PM



2525 Noble Rd
Raleigh NC 27608



919-996-3135



Kiwanis Park

item.title: Celebrate Play, Art, and Community at Caraleigh Park (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Enjoy free cold treats, sensory-friendly activities, music, and visit with public artist, Jean Gray Mohs.



Project made possible by an Accessibility for Parks Grant. For more information, visit the Caraleigh Park Improvements Project page.



Wednesday, July 1, from 4 - 6 p.m.  
110 Maywood Avenue, Raleigh, NC 27603



RSVP preferred but not required by Wednesday, June 24
Email: RSVP@raleighnc.gov

item.title: Welcome to Summer (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Come celebrate with us on June 9 for an evening packed with carnival games, face painting, delicious food, arts & crafts, and more. Bring the whole family, fun is guaranteed for all ages! Registration is encouraged. Let’s start the summer off right!



Location: Worthdale Community Center
Date: Tuesday, June 9
Time: 5 - 7 p.m.
Ages: 5 -12
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Art Classes at Five Points Center for Active Adults (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Do you make your art independently, yet sometimes yearn for feedback from an objective eye? Open Studio with Adrienne provides an environment for your fully independent process with available feedback when requested and occasional scheduled group “crits”. Adrienne Garnett has years of teaching visual art in NY public schools and coaching graduate and undergraduate art teachers in their college colloquium. Six sessions to begin with. Bring your own materials.



Dates: Friday, Aug. 21 - Sept. 25
Time: 2:30 - 4:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $25
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks and Blue Cross NC Partner to Fuel Summer Fun with Expanded Meal Support (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks is partnering with Blue Cross and Blue Shield of North Carolina (Blue Cross NC) to expand access to nutritious meals for children attending summer camps—helping ensure that every child has what they need to play, learn, and thrive during the summer months. This support is especially critical for families who may lose access to school-based meal programs once the academic year ends.



Through this partnership, Blue Cross NC will fund 6,000 meals across three weeks of summer camps, supporting children in neighborhoods most affected by food insecurity. Raleigh Parks will continue to provide meals throughout the season, serving thousands of campers and helping reduce financial barriers for families.



“This partnership with Blue Cross NC helps us ensure more Raleigh kids have the nutritious meals they need to enjoy summer camp to the fullest. We're grateful for partners who help us strengthen our impact for local families,” says Darrell Kain, Community Engagement Supervisor for Raleigh Parks, Recreation and Cultural Resources.



This collaboration reflects a shared commitment to child wellness and access to healthy food. By investing in summer nutrition, Blue Cross NC helps ensure campers stay fueled for active play, social connection, and memorable summer experiences.

item.title: Memoir and Personal Essay Writing Workshop (Jun 1, 2026)
item.description:
It can be funny, touching, or life-changing. Whether you know what you'd like to write about or you need help finding it, this class will have various tools and exercises to help you along the way. 

At the end of 6 weeks, all students will have a 2-3 page story that is personal to them, and a wonderful treasure to share with loved ones.

Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Wednesdays, July 8 - Aug. 12 
Time: 11 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $18 Residents / $33 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Movie Series at Pullen Park (May 29, 2026)
item.description:
Families, friends, and film lovers of all ages are invited to bring blankets and lawn chairs for a season of outdoor cinema on the event lawn. Screenings will take place on the first Tuesday of every month, kicking off in June and concluding in October.



All screenings are free and open to the public. Refreshments and popcorn will be available for purchase at the Pullen Café.



Please note that film screenings are contingent upon weather conditions; news of cancellations will be updated on the Raleigh Parks website.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Ages: All
Cost: Free
